b:T4e70,<div class="specification">
<p>Enzyme activity is affected by temperature and pH.</p>
<p style="text-align: center;"><img src="data:image/png;base64,"/></p>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>Explain the decrease in activity of the enzyme on either side of the optimum temperature.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;">
<p>In biotechnology, enzymes are used to transfer genes to bacteria. Outline how <strong>two</strong> specific enzymes are used for the transfer.</p>
<div class="marks">[2]</div>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>c:T4d9,<div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<ol style="list-style-type:lower-alpha;">
<li>as temperature rises/is higher (than optimal temperature), the enzyme is denatured;</li>
<li>as the temperature drops the enzyme molecules have less kinetic energy<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>fewer successful collisions;</li>
</ol>
<p><em>OWTTE</em></p>
<div class="question_part_label">a.</div>
</div><div class="question" style="padding-left: 20px;">
<ol style="list-style-type:lower-alpha;">
<li>restriction enzymes/(restriction) endonucleases cut the gene and the bacterial/plasmid/vector DNA in the same/specific restriction sites<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>(restriction) endonucleases work by targeting a specific sequence of base pairs in DNA causing both strands of the DNA to break apart;</li>
<li>(DNA) ligase attaches/inserts the gene to the bacterial/plasmid/vector DNA<br/><em><strong>OR</strong></em><br/>(DNA) ligase joins the vector and gene by fusing their sugar-phosphate backbones together (with a covalent phosphodiester bond);</li>
<li>correct reference to reverse transcriptase;</li>
</ol>
<p><em>Accept correct mention of reverse transcriptase</em>.</p>
<div class="question_part_label">b.</div>
</div>2:["$","article",null,{"className":"q-page","children":[["$","p",null,{"style":{"margin":0},"children":["$","$L6",null,{"href":"/subject/biology/topic/2/","children":["← back to ","Biology"," topic ","2"]}]}],["$","h1",null,{"children":"22M.2.SL.TZ2.5"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-meta","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"tag","children":"pestle"}],["$","span",null,{"children":"Biology"}],null,["$","span",null,{"children":"SL"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["Paper ","2"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"22M"}],["$","span",null,{"children":["· ","2-5-enzymes"]}],["$","a",null,{"href":"https://pestle.pages.dev/app/","target":"_blank","rel":"noopener","children":"source ↗"}]]}],["$","div",null,{"className":"q-content","dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$b"}}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Markscheme / solution"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"$c"}}]]}],["$","details",null,{"className":"solution","children":[["$","summary",null,{"children":"Examiners’ report"}],["$","div",null,{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n<p>In a. many incorrectly stated that the enzyme was denatured either side of the optimum. Denaturation was reasonably well known, but the explanation for increased activity with rising temperature was not. Surprisingly some candidates missed the obvious references to temperature and wrote about pH instead. Knowledge of the enzymes involved in gene transfer was very patchy, with over a quarter of candidates leaving it blank. This was the worst scoring question on the paper.</p>\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">a.</div>\n</div><div class=\"question\" style=\"padding-left: 20px;\">\n[N/A]\n<div class=\"question_part_label\">b.</div>\n</div>"}}]]}]]}]
